meanings.definition: the act of participating in an athletic competition in which you must jump
the act of jumping; propelling yourself off the ground
examples: he advanced in a series of jumps

meanings.definition: move forward by leaps and bounds
examples: The horse bounded across the meadow
The child leapt across the puddle
Can you jump over the fence?

meanings.definition: move or jump suddenly, as if in surprise or alarm
examples: She startled when I walked into the room

meanings.definition: make a sudden physical attack on
examples: The muggers jumped the woman in the fur coat
Her new novel jumped high on the bestseller list

meanings.definition: jump down from an elevated point
examples: the parachutist didn't want to jump
every year, hundreds of people jump off the Golden Gate bridge
the widow leapt into the funeral pyre

meanings.definition: run off or leave the rails
examples: the train derailed because a cow was standing on the tracks

meanings.definition: jump from an airplane and descend with a parachute

examples: the trainer jumped the tiger through the hoop

meanings.definition: start (a car engine whose battery is dead) by connecting it to another car's battery

examples: He skipped a row in the text and so the sentence was incomprehensible

meanings.definition: pass abruptly from one state or topic to another
examples: jump from one thing to another

meanings.definition: go back and forth; swing back and forth between two states or conditions
